His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62472755

62472818
>>62461988
Everest didn’t randomly integrate Stellar — they built on it deliberately because Stellar is positioned to become the compliant public blockchain backbone for the tokenized economy, and Everest is engineering the regulated on-ramps/wallets/payments layer to capture it. Here’s the maximalist, everything-aligns thesis:
1. Strategic Positioning at the Perfect Intersection
• Everest = MiCA-licensed (EU passport), compliant custody, payments, fiat on/off-ramps, and now native Stellar wallet generation (BIP39 in EverWallet via SCF Kickstart Award).
• Stellar = Fast, cheap, compliance-native (clawbacks, authorized trustlines, etc.), with massive institutional validation via DTCC.
• The Kickstart Award isn’t just funding — it’s SDF officially stamping Everest as a key builder. This opens doors to deeper integration, more grants, co-marketing, and priority support as Stellar scales.
Everest becomes the regulated European gateway for Stellar flows: EU users/institutions access DTCC-tokenized assets (stocks, ETFs, Treasuries), stablecoins, and cross-border payments seamlessly through EverWallet.

62472822
>>62472818
DTCC connecting its tokenization service to Stellar (DTC assets live H1 2027) is one of the biggest TradFi-crypto bridges ever. We’re talking infrastructure for quadrillions in annual settlement volume potentially touching a public chain.
• Tokenized RWAs on Stellar need compliant wallets, custody, transfers, and EU distribution.
• Everest’s MiCA license + Stellar-native wallet = plug-and-play solution for European participants. No need for institutions to build everything themselves.
• Result: Explosive volume on Stellar higher XLM demand (fees, liquidity, bridges) + massive utility for Everest’s platform.
This isn’t payments-only anymore. It’s Everest sitting at the intersection of Wall Street tokenization and EU-regulated access.
3. Tokenized Economy Flywheel
• MiCA Advantage: Everest is already authorized for custody, transfers, etc. As MiCA matures and tokenized securities/stablecoins boom in Europe, Everest white-labels solutions for merchants, fintechs, and institutions.
• Wallet + Payments Moat: EverWallet with seamless Stellar (plus BTC/SOL roadmap) becomes a multi-chain compliant hub. Users generate Stellar addresses easily, hold tokenized assets, send remittances, swap, earn — all regulated.
• Network Effects: More assets on Stellar (DTCC + others) more users need compliant interfaces Everest captures fees, custody spreads, and ecosystem partnerships. SCF validation accelerates this.

62472827
>>62472822
• Stellar TVL and volume surge from DTCC flows XLM utility and price re-rate significantly (institutional adoption narrative goes parabolic).
• Everest, as an early regulated builder with SDF backing, sees user growth, revenue from services, and potential token ($ID) or valuation upside as the “Stellar-compliant layer in Europe.”
• Multi-chain expansion (already hinting BTC/SOL) positions them beyond one network while Stellar remains the core for real-world compliant assets.
• Timing: 2026–2027 window is when pilots go live. Everest’s Kickstart work is shipping now, giving them first-mover polish in wallets/UI.
Why They Chose Stellar “For a Reason”: Low costs + speed for payments/remittances, built-in compliance primitives perfect for regulated tokenized securities, and now direct DTCC pipeline. Ethereum is great for DeFi but heavier/costlier for high-volume settlement. Stellar is the efficient, institutional-friendly public chain.
In the super scenario: DTCC/Stellar becomes the standard for tokenized securities. Everest rides it as the go-to EU-compliant wallet/custody/payments layer. XLM reprices for infrastructure status. Everest scales into a major fintech player in the tokenized economy, with strong revenue, user base, and ecosystem gravity.
This is the aligned, non-random bet: regulation + infrastructure + timing + grants + institutional tailwinds. High conviction setup if the DTCC integration delivers.

62473294
>>62472818
>>62472822
>>62472827

62473379
>>62473294

item.title: I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking. — 17 replies — 68.0/hr

I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-12 21:06 UTC

OP

I am fully mentally finished, motivationally speaking.

I'm an ugly beta male in my mid 30s. My social life has been non-existent for my entire adult life. I missed out on any socialising during university and my 20s and afterwards. All of human society is built for normies and ugly beta males like me are seen as shit.

My job is pointless and WFH. I'm overpaid and any sideways move will lead to worse living conditions and commuting. I'm too ugly and non-normie to ever be promoted.

Culturally, my time has passed. I have recently gained the habit of pining over playing 90s and 00s video games, and then being bored to death when actually playing them. I played video games a lot in my youth, then barely any in my 20s, and a bit more in recent years. But it's all pointless.

I have produced nothing. I have no skills. I am a viewer of lives. I missed out on the web 2.0 boom, the crypto boom, every boom, every trend. The things I do, reading and going to the gym and walking, feel like procrastination. I have never taken anything to its full conclusion. I have never put sustained effort into anything.

item.title: Why the 2025 crypto run was so shit? 10/10/25 absolutely gutted it but even before that happened the run was unsuccessfu… — 14 replies — 49.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why the 2025 crypto run was so shit? 10/10/25 absolutely gutted it but even before that happened the run was unsuccessfu…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-12 20:55 UTC

OP

Why the 2025 crypto run was so shit? 10/10/25 absolutely gutted it but even before that happened the run was unsuccessful, but why? I don't think I quite get it.
The whales funded media promotion per usual, influencers, social media marketing, articles, fake narratives (like GENIUS act hype disinfo) - it seems it was a real effort on their part (at least initially).

Two explanations that kinda make sense (not really) if you don't think about it for too long are:
>Explanation 1: crypto got cucked by AI hype, AI moves, AI bullrun
Crypto whales and insiders couldn't possibly not know that. But they deployed the usual pro-crypto bamboozle campaign and kept it going for a long time regardless. They knew it will be a bad run, but invested in it and deployed it anyway? Why? Why not wait?

>Explanation 2: the run was supposed to be bigger, but the pleb didn't arrive
It also doesn't make much sense. Pleb is attracted by big gains, fake hype narratives and promise of quick easy money. There was none of that during this run. No alt season. No flashy, attention capturing +1000% runs of silly shitcoins. BTC couldn't even beat gold. ATHs were broken by insignificant %.
And whenever the pleb started to arrive they were scared off by sudden huge dumps and coordinated FUD campaigns.
The explanation of "we tried, but the pleb didn't arrive" is not valid.

So why the 2025 run was so shit? What was this all about? I genuinely would like to know.

From my limited knowledge the market attack was a planned, coordinated move, coming from either wallstreet or binance (or binance was contracted by wallstreet to perform it). It's kind of surprising that no major exchanges shut down afterwards, at least yet.

10/10/25 is the biggest event since FTX shutdown (binance also played a huge part in it), but the 2025 bullrun was shit even before 10/10/25.

>>62470991
A trick that worked for me is that I went to a temp agency, you don't need a resume for that, they hire anyone. You show up any day you want and maybe work that day, if something comes up they give you a brief description of the job and you can accept or decline. You work that day and get paid that day. All kinds of various random jobs, construction, picking up garbage around a mall, sorting the metal out of demolition debris, sweeping floors etc. You get all kinds of things to put on a resume and the variety makes it seem like you've been working for years. Work there for a few weeks or months and then lie about how long you've been doing those jobs to fill the gap. If the employer calls them about your employment history there, they will know who you are and vouch for you. Also the freelance nature of it makes it easier to ease into working after being NEET for so long. The downside is that you sometimes work with literal hobos or tweakers.

62472415
>>62472062
I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin. New Link that enters circulation comes not only from node operators selling but Chainlink Labs themselves selling tokens. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

62472614
>>62472415
>miners decide the price of BTC
You're legitimately retarded.

62472624
>>62472415
>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

62472630
>>62472062
>>62466927
>Some anon did the math in another thread;
Link to thread?

62472819

Obviously I meant for us to track it

62472871
>>62472614
Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC

Holy shit yes they are.

yeah this is definitely wrong lmao

That's exactly how all crypto inflationary rewards work.
For example: 95% of Bitcoin mining rewards are inflationary, and only like 5% have to be bought by users from the open market.

And in the case of Link, much of it is being bought on the open market via payment abstraction.

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

love your attitude op so i've come to bring you knowledge that yall may already know. i'm almost at the 6 figure table.

1. live with your parents, you losers are probably already doing this. with minimal living expenses you can invest more. also allows you to drop the emergency fund which again lets you invest more.

2. be young, youth is an advantage for compounding interest. a huge advantage.

3. get a job, yes a wage slave. obviously a better job will allow you to invest more. it won't be forever. the sooner you hit the rpg grind irl the sooner you can spend the rest of your life eating hot pockets and playing vidia.

4. invest in growth etf's. your crypto is fools gold. yes the stock market can crash but it always bounces back even if it takes years to do it. and you all are young enough to ride out the bad years. once you get old, or have enough then sell your growth etf and get dividend etfs and live the good life.

5. you can't just invest in safe etf's but you have to place them in the right location.

5a) 401k up to employer match, this is the best because its free money for you but the investments are usually not the best. if you have the option get the ROTH 401k not the normal one if the company offers it. for a normal 401k what you put in is tax differed, when you draw from it at 59 1/2 then you pay taxes on it.

5b) ROTH ira is you own personal retirement account. you pay taxes on what you put in but no taxes on what grows inside or when you pull your money out. 59 1/2 also. has a limit on how much you can invest into it each year so max it.

5c) health savings account, hsa. what you contribute can be deducted from your taxes, what grows in it not taxed and when you spend it on a medical expense its not taxed. if you save your bills you can reimburse yourself at a later date once the account grows huge. at 65 you can just withdraw from the account as if it was income.

im just some old fag trying to help my younger brothers.

Rockets launched from Wenchang fall into the sea, not onto populated areas. It's the rockets launched from Xichang that fall onto populated areas. This is because the location for Xichang spaceport was chosen during the Cold War when security from military strike was considered very important. There are no unpopulated in the Chinese interior at the low latitudes from which you can launch to geosynchronous orbit. Xichang spaceport will probably be closed within 5 years, or at most 10 years.
